name: Encapsulation

forces: Freedom to change internals ↔ stable public API
x.structure: Bundle data with the operations that maintain its invariants, and expose only a controlled interface; internal representation can change behind it.
antiPatterns: Anemic objects (data with no behaviour) + external manipulation
Getters/setters that expose every field, defeating the boundary
x.scale: Class / module level; the basis of object-oriented design and module boundaries.

def: Bundling state with the behaviour that guards its invariants, exposing a controlled interface while hiding the representation.
problem: When external code touches an object’s raw state, invariants can be broken and every internal change becomes a breaking change.
mechanism: Make state private and mediate all access through methods that preserve invariants. The public interface becomes the contract; the representation is free to evolve behind it.
benefits: Internals can change without breaking callers
limits: Easy to fake with getter/setter pairs
failures: "Encapsulation" via full getter/setter exposure (no real boundary)
Anemic models with logic scattered outside
myths: "Getters and setters = encapsulation" — exposing all fields defeats the point
